cover_narration: Welcome back... In this walkthrough, we'll create a logical data model from scratch using Quest Data Modeler... Let's get started.
outro_narration: And that's it... You've successfully created a logical data model with entities and relationships... Thanks for watching... and see you in the next demo.

steps.label: Wait for the Overview page
steps.narration: We begin on the Overview page...

steps.label: Locate the Quick Actions row
steps.narration: And look for the Quick Actions section...

steps.label: Open the New Model dialog
steps.narration: From here, click on New Model to begin.

steps.label: Wait for the New Model dialog
steps.narration: Once the dialog appears...

steps.label: Name the model with a fresh timestamp
steps.narration: Enter a name for your model... We'll use a timestamped name to keep things unique...

steps.label: Select Logical model type
steps.narration: Next... select Logical Model as the model type...

steps.label: Click Create to open the modeling canvas
steps.narration: And click Create.

steps.label: Wait for the diagram editor to load
steps.narration: Give it a moment... as the modeling canvas loads...

steps.label: Activate the Add Entity tool
steps.narration: Now, let's start building our model. Click on the Add Entity tool...

steps.label: Tool ready — cursor in entity-placement mode
Place the first entity on the canvas
steps.narration: And place your first entity anywhere on the canvas.

steps.label: Wait for the New Entity dialog
steps.narration: In the dialog that appears...

steps.label: Name the entity Customer
steps.narration: Enter the name Customer...

steps.label: Commit the entity name (blur)
steps.narration: Then press Tab...

steps.label: Click outside the dialog to close it
steps.narration: And click outside to confirm.

steps.label: Wait for canvas to update
steps.narration: Great... the first entity is now created.

steps.label: Activate Add Entity tool again
steps.narration: Let's add another one. Activate the Add Entity tool again...

steps.label: Place the second entity on the canvas
steps.narration: And place a second entity on the canvas.

steps.label: Wait for the second New Entity dialog
Name the entity Orders
steps.narration: This time... name it Orders...

steps.label: Commit the second entity name
steps.narration: Press Tab to confirm...

steps.label: Click outside to close the second dialog
steps.narration: And click outside to close the dialog.

steps.label: Two entities placed on the canvas
steps.narration: Now we have two entities in our model... Customer... and Orders...

steps.label: Activate the Add Relationships tool
steps.narration: Next, let's define the relationship between them. Select the Add Relationships tool...

steps.label: Relationship tool ready
Drag from Customer to Orders to draw a relationship
steps.narration: And drag from Customer to Orders.

steps.label: Wait for the relationship line to render
steps.narration: Wait a moment... and you'll see the relationship line appear.

steps.label: Switch back to the Select tool
steps.narration: Once done... switch back to the Select tool.

steps.label: Open the Mart save menu
steps.narration: Finally... let's save our model. Click on the Mart menu...

steps.label: Wait for the Mart menu to render
steps.narration: Wait for the options to load...

steps.label: Choose Save as
steps.narration: And choose Save As.

steps.label: Wait for the Save as dialog
steps.narration: The save dialog will appear... and your model is now ready to be stored.
